title: [lora_l2.c:723] lichen_lora_l2_deinit has TOCTOU race in state check
description:
Contract: lichen_lora_l2_deinit should atomically transition from STOPPED/ABORTED to DEINITING.
Violation: Lines 725-753 read state, then check various conditions with if/else-if chain, creating a window between state read and lora_transition_from() calls. A concurrent thread could change state in this window (though unlikely in embedded context).
Impact: Low in practice - embedded systems typically single-threaded for this type of operation. The CAS in lora_transition_from() catches most races. However, the pattern is not consistent with the atomic discipline used elsewhere.
Mitigation: The existing lora_transition_from() CAS provides sufficient protection. Document that concurrent deinit() calls are not supported.
close_reason: False positive: while the code reads state then checks it (TOCTOU pattern), lora_transition_from() uses atomic_cas() internally which atomically verifies the expected state. If state changes between lora_get_state() and the CAS, the CAS fails safely. The initial read is just an optimization to select which expected-state to try. Added SECURITY comment documenting why this is safe.
